mnemonics: The Collection Family: List (ordered, allows duplicates), Set (no duplicates), Map (key-value pairs). Remember 'Lots of Stuff, Some Eat Mangoes' for List, Set, Map.
keyPoints: Collection is the root interface for most collection types (List, Set, Queue).
Map is a separate interface from Collection and stores key-value pairs.
Understand the core differences: Lists are ordered and allow duplicates, Sets are unordered and forbid duplicates, Maps are key-based.
commonMistakes: Treating a Map as a Collection (it is not a direct subtype).
Assuming all collections are ordered (only Lists and LinkedHashSet are).
Using == instead of .equals() to compare objects within collections.
